name: Al Hirt
abstract: Alois Maxwell "Al" Hirt (November 7, 1922 – April 27, 1999) was an American trumpeter and bandleader. He is best remembered for his million-selling recordings of "Java" and the accompanying album Honey in the Horn (1963), and for the theme music to The Green Hornet.

concerts.label: Al Hirt and his All-Star Sextet
Al Hirt and his All-Star Sextet
Al Hirt, Trumpet, and New Orleans Music Festival

works.t: Is It True What They Say About Dixie?

about_quotes.speaker: Ellis Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: I think the first band that I played with which could be considered an integrated band was when I started playing with Al Hirt.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ellis Marsalis interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Orleans, Louisiana, January 7, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Havens
about_quotes.evidence: Al had started a band... he and Pete Fountain and I were the original members of the front line of this band in 1957.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Havens interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Ascione
about_quotes.evidence: When I got to play with Jay McShann and Cab Calloway and Al Hirt and Milt Hinton and all those guys.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Ascione interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Strazzeri
about_quotes.evidence: We used to play local gigs. I remember playing with Al, I used to play high school dances with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Strazzeri interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Avakian George
about_quotes.evidence: The transcript mentions 'Al Hirt' only in the prompt's 'PERSON TO JUDGE' field. The interviewee (George Avakian) discusses his brother, Al Avakian (also called Al), extensively, but there is no mention of the trumpeter Al Hirt in the provided text.
about_quotes.doc_title: Avakian George — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Snooky Young and Gerald Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: I worked, I did two concerts at Carnegie Hall with Al Hirt, and Snooky was on the band then, and J.J. Johnson, we had all those great guys there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Snooky Young and Gerald Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995
